title: FULL OUTER JOIN
content:
### FULL OUTER JOIN

FULL OUTER JOIN returns ALL rows from both tables. When a match exists, rows are combined. When there is no match, NULL values fill the missing side.

---

### FULL OUTER JOIN Syntax

```sql
SELECT columns
FROM table1
FULL OUTER JOIN table2 ON table1.column = table2.column;
```

The OUTER keyword is optional — FULL JOIN is the same as FULL OUTER JOIN.

### FULL OUTER JOIN Example

```sql
-- All employees and all departments, matched where possible
SELECT e.name, d.dept_name
FROM employees e
FULL OUTER JOIN departments d ON e.dept_id = d.id;

-- Employees without a department: name shown, dept_name is NULL
-- Departments without employees: dept_name shown, name is NULL
```

### Finding Unmatched on Both Sides

```sql
-- Rows that don't have a match on either side
SELECT e.name, d.dept_name
FROM employees e
FULL OUTER JOIN departments d ON e.dept_id = d.id
WHERE e.id IS NULL OR d.id IS NULL;
```

### Database Support

| Database | FULL OUTER JOIN |
|----------|----------------|
| PostgreSQL | Supported |
| SQL Server | Supported |
| Oracle | Supported |
| MySQL | Not supported (use UNION of LEFT and RIGHT JOIN) |
| SQLite | Not supported |

### Simulating FULL OUTER JOIN in MySQL

```sql
SELECT e.name, d.dept_name
FROM employees e
LEFT JOIN departments d ON e.dept_id = d.id
UNION
SELECT e.name, d.dept_name
FROM employees e
RIGHT JOIN departments d ON e.dept_id = d.id;
```

> **Key Point:** FULL OUTER JOIN is less common than INNER or LEFT JOIN. It is most useful when you need to see all records from both tables and identify gaps on either side.
codeExample:
SELECT e.name AS employee_name,
       d.dept_name AS department
FROM employees e
FULL OUTER JOIN departments d ON e.dept_id = d.id
ORDER BY e.name;

quiz.question: FULL OUTER JOIN returns:
options: All rows from the left table only
All rows from the right table only

quiz.question: Which major database does NOT support FULL OUTER JOIN?
